repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Closes #39209

## Problem

There is no built-in text object for operating on the whole buffer. #39209 settled on `l`: `il` is useful as a charwise current-line object, while `al` is useful as a linewise whole-buffer object.

## Solution

Add line (`l`) text objects:

- `al`: select the whole buffer linewise.
- `il`: select the current line without leading/trailing whitespace or the line break.

`il` fails (does nothing) on blank/whitespace-only lines. Visual mode, `selection=exclusive`, and multibyte chars are correctly considered. jumplist unaffacted.

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -485,6 +485,7 @@ Tag		Command		   Op-pending and Visual-mode action	~
 |v_a]|		a]		   same as a[
 |v_a`|		a`		   string in backticks
 |v_ab|		ab		   "a block" from "[(" to "])" (with braces)
+|v_al|		al		   select all lines

@@ -503,6 +504,7 @@ Tag		Command		   Op-pending and Visual-mode action	~
 |v_i]|		i]		   same as i[
 |v_i`|		i`		   string in backticks without the backticks
 |v_ib|		ib		   "inner block" from "[(" to "])"
+|v_il|		il		   inner line

comment_data.body: could add `test/functional/editor/textobject_spec.lua` to keep the oldtests "pure", since it's unlikely that Vim will accept this upstream
done, will be less hesitant to make new files and whatnot as I intuitied it didn't make sense to do this and extend `old/`. thanks zeertzjq and justin

+|v_al|		al		  all lines (entire buffer)

comment_data.body:
does "line break" mean EOL here? that is not really how vim docs would say it. elsewhere in this file it mentions `End-of-line` or `<EOL>`. 

But I'd suggest not mentioning this at all, since it's [understood](https://github.com/barrettruth/neovim/blob/b44c2bdd16226f6caa5324d91f1ae9781ffdc12b/runtime/doc/motion.txt#L528-L531) for `i` textobjects, and it's also redundant with the preceding sentence ("without whitespace").



```suggestion
il			"inner line", select the current line without leading
			or trailing white space.  Fails on a blank or white-space-only line.
```

comment_regular_data.body:
Thank you for working on this! I tested it myself and it seems to work perfectly, with one notable exception (see below). I unfortunately can't review the C implementation myself since I don't know C.

This implementation of `o_al` incorrectly moves the cursor. Compare to this implementation, which does not move the cursor:

```lua
vim.keymap.set('o', 'al', function()
  local pos = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(0)
  vim.cmd('keepjumps norm! gg' .. vim.v.operator .. 'G')
  vim.api.nvim_win_set_cursor(0, pos)
end)
```

Edit: Also, I didn't check if your implementation preserves the jumplist, but I imagine it does.

Would it be possible for `il` to support count like `$`, `g_`, and `_` do? It could then be possible, like with those text objects, to use `[count-1]` to grow the selection.

In the case of a count > 1 selection, the new lines in-between would be preserved, but the trailing EOL would still be ignored (similar to `g_` over multiple lines).

> possible for `il` to support count like `$`, `g_`, and `_` do?

Let's do that in a followup PR

title: feat(normal): add line (`l`) text objects
